assumptions: The question is about hedge accounting for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognised intercompany monetary balance for purposes of identifying relevant hedge accounting models.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including certain intragroup transactions in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as the separate hedge accounting model for group foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.
